title: hieracium murorum subsp. divisum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. divisum, also known as the wall hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the United Kingdom. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to help with wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. divisum include its ability to support the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
